free_text: Production of medium-energy neutrons from proton
bombardment of light elements.
Chico State College, Chico, California.
Data normalized to new n-p data
old n-p
data set
old n-p
data set
new n-p data set
new n-p data set
76-Inches cyclotron.
.Absolute proton beam current was measured by a
faraday cup.
0.38- Or 0.76-mm thick polyethylene converters
were used to produce recoil protons for the purposes
of neutron spectrum measurements and relative beam
monitoring.
A telescope of two plastic
scintillators and a sodium Iodide Counter- Variant 1
A Delta-E-E Si(Li) detector
telescope was also used to measure the recoil particle
energy-Variant 2
One of variants 1 or 2 was used in different runs. The
telescope was placed at 24 degree. to the beam axis.
The similar telescope placed at 20 Degree to the beam
axis was used for relative beam monitoring
Recoil protons produced by neutrons in a thin
polyethylene converter were detected.
.Uncertainty in the incident proton energy.
Similar data are given for deuterium, lithium-6
and beryllium, carbon and aluminum targets.
By Khlopin Radium Institute - A.P.
Misprints in Bib Section Corrected - A.P.
Date is Corrected
Subentries O0654004,011-013 are corrected
according to O.Schwerer's remarks.
.008-013: Data renormalized to new (n,p)
data at 132deg(c.m.) published in the 1976 article
adopted.
Lithium Foil. Isotopic Composition is not Given. The
Foil Thickness Corresponds to an Energy Loss of 1.12
MeV For 39.3 MeV Protons.

Integration over the high-energy neutron peak.
Performed: For losses due to nuclear interactions in
the proton recoil detectors - a few per-cent;
For multiple scattering of the protons in the neutron
production target that influences the faraday cup
measurements.
Quoted errors are due to statistics
combined with uncertainties in target thickness
determination, energy calibration and solid angles
subtended by the neutron collimator and the proton
telescope.

- By Authors - In the case the value given excludes

- By Authors - Average cross sections for neutron
production from deuterium in 5 MeV intervals measured
from the peak cross-section energy.  The 0-5 MeV
interval includes all the peak.
Quoted errors are due to statistics
combined with uncertainties in target thickness
determination, energy calibration and solid angles
subtended by the neutron collimator and the proton
telescope.

-  By  Authors - The average cross section over a 2 MeV
energy interval is reported.  Cross sections are not
given for the case of 29.4 MeV Incident Protons Because
of the large negative Q ( - 18.4 MEV) of the (P,N)
reaction on Carbon 12.
